questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What must you do at a steady red traffic light?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Slow down and proceed if the way looks clear
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Treat it the same as a yield sign
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires a full stop, and you must wait until the light turns green before proceeding.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing red traffic light mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop, then proceed when safe, as at a stop sign
Slow down but you do not need to stop
The signal is broken and should be ignored
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated exactly like a stop sign: stop fully, then go when it is safe.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light indicate?
choices: The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
You have the right-of-way to speed through
Traffic from the side street must stop
questions.explanation: A yellow light warns that red is coming, so you should stop when it is safe rather than accelerate.

questions.prompt: What does an octagonal (eight-sided) sign always mean?
questions.explanation: The octagon shape is used only for stop signs, so you can recognize it even if the word is obscured.

questions.prompt: What does a solid yellow center line on your side of the road mean?
choices: Do not cross it to pass
Passing is allowed in both directions
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited there.

questions.prompt: A downward-pointing triangular sign means:
questions.explanation: The inverted-triangle shape is reserved for yield signs.

questions.prompt: What color and shape are most warning signs that alert you to hazards ahead?
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond-shaped signs warn of hazards or changing conditions ahead.

questions.prompt: A broken (dashed) white line between lanes means:
choices: You may change lanes when it is safe
You may never cross the line
questions.explanation: A broken white line separates lanes going the same direction and allows lane changes when safe.

questions.prompt: What does a green arrow signal displayed with a red light allow?
choices: You may proceed in the direction of the arrow
You must stop in all directions
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected movement in the arrow's direction while other traffic is held.

questions.prompt: When a traffic signal is completely dark due to a power failure, you should:
choices: Treat the intersection as an all-way stop
Speed up to get through quickly
questions.explanation: A dark signal should be handled as a four-way stop, with drivers taking turns based on arrival order.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, who has the right-of-way when two vehicles arrive at the same time?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach an all-way stop simultaneously, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When making a left turn, you must:
choices: Yield to oncoming traffic that is close enough to be a hazard
Proceed first because left turns have priority
Stop only if a sign requires it
questions.explanation: Left-turning drivers must yield to oncoming vehicles that are close enough to pose a danger.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a crosswalk?
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians within a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout
Stop and wait for a green light
Make all turns to the left
questions.explanation: Vehicles already circulating in a roundabout have the right-of-way over those entering.

questions.prompt: A driver entering a roadway from a private driveway or alley must:
choices: Yield to all traffic already on the road
Proceed because driveway traffic has priority
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering from driveways, alleys, or private roads must yield to traffic on the main roadway.

questions.prompt: When you hear a siren and see an approaching emergency vehicle, you should:
choices: Pull over to the right and stop until it passes
Speed up to get out of the way
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you signal your intention?
choices: At least 100 feet before the turn
Only as you begin the turn
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet ahead gives other drivers time to react to your turn.

questions.prompt: When you make a right turn, you should turn into:
choices: The lane nearest the right curb
questions.explanation: Right turns should be made close to the curb into the nearest right-hand lane.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection with no signs or signals, you should:
choices: Yield to vehicles already in the intersection and to the driver on your right
Stop and wait for a signal to appear
questions.explanation: Without controls, yield to traffic already there and to the vehicle approaching from your right.

questions.prompt: Before turning, you should move into the correct lane:
choices: Only after you enter the intersection
questions.explanation: Getting into the proper lane early makes your turn predictable and safe.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the speed limit in a Nevada residential or business district?
questions.explanation: Nevada sets a default of 25 mph in residential and business districts when no other limit is posted.

questions.prompt: Nevada's basic speed law requires you to:
choices: Never drive faster than is reasonable and safe for conditions
Always drive the posted limit regardless of weather
Drive as fast as traffic allows
Match the speed of the slowest vehicle
questions.explanation: Even at or below the posted limit, you must adjust speed to road, weather, and traffic conditions.

questions.prompt: What is a safe minimum following distance in good conditions?
choices: Three seconds behind the vehicle ahead
One car length at any speed
questions.explanation: The three-second rule gives you enough time to react and stop in normal conditions.

questions.prompt: In rain, snow, or fog, your following distance should be:
choices: Increased to four seconds or more
Kept the same as in dry weather
questions.explanation: Slippery or low-visibility conditions require extra space to stop safely.

questions.prompt: Total stopping distance is made up of:
choices: Only the distance you see ahead
questions.explanation: Stopping distance combines how far you travel while reacting and how far you travel while braking.

questions.prompt: If you double your speed, your braking distance:
questions.explanation: Braking distance grows roughly with the square of speed, so doubling speed quadruples it.

questions.prompt: When traveling much slower than surrounding traffic, you should:
choices: Speed up to match traffic regardless of the limit
questions.explanation: Slower vehicles should stay right so faster traffic can pass safely on the left.

questions.prompt: When approaching a school zone during posted hours, you should:
choices: Slow to the posted reduced speed and watch for children
Speed up to clear the area
Ignore the limit if no children are visible
questions.explanation: Reduced school-zone limits protect children, so slow down and stay alert during those hours.

questions.prompt: If another driver is tailgating you, the safest response is to:
choices: Increase your following distance from the vehicle ahead and let them pass
Speed up well over the limit
questions.explanation: Leaving more space ahead gives you a cushion so you can brake gradually, and allowing the tailgater to pass removes the risk.

questions.prompt: Driving too slowly on a highway is dangerous because it:
choices: Can impede traffic and cause crashes
questions.explanation: Driving far below the flow of traffic can obstruct other vehicles and increase crash risk.

questions.prompt: You should NOT pass another vehicle when:
choices: A solid yellow line is on your side of the road
The road ahead is clear and straight
A dashed line is on your side
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side marks a no-passing zone.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, the left lane is generally intended for:
questions.explanation: Keep right except to pass; the left lane is for passing and faster traffic.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal and check your mirrors and blind spot
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking mirrors plus your blind spot prevents collisions with vehicles you cannot see.

questions.prompt: When another vehicle is passing you, you should:
choices: Maintain or slightly reduce your speed
Speed up to prevent the pass
questions.explanation: Holding or easing your speed lets the passing driver complete the maneuver safely.

questions.prompt: Passing is generally prohibited:
choices: On hills and curves where you cannot see ahead
Whenever you are in a hurry
questions.explanation: You should never pass where limited sight distance prevents you from seeing oncoming traffic.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
You are even with its front bumper
questions.explanation: Seeing the vehicle in your mirror confirms you have enough room to return without cutting it off.

questions.prompt: The proper way to check a blind spot before changing lanes is to:
questions.explanation: Mirrors cannot show the blind spot, so a quick shoulder check is necessary.

questions.prompt: Two solid yellow lines in the center of the road mean:
questions.explanation: Double solid yellow lines prohibit passing for traffic in both directions.

questions.prompt: You may pass on the right:
choices: When the vehicle ahead is turning left and there is room
On any shoulder at any time
questions.explanation: Passing on the right is allowed only in limited situations, such as when a vehicle ahead is turning left and a lane is available.

questions.prompt: When driving on a road with several lanes in your direction, you should:
choices: Choose one lane and avoid weaving between lanes
Switch lanes frequently to stay ahead
Drive in two lanes at once
questions.explanation: Staying in one lane and changing only when needed makes your driving predictable and safe.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist in Nevada, you must leave at least:
choices: Three feet of space or move to an adjacent lane
Six feet of space at all times
questions.explanation: Nevada law requires moving over a lane when possible, or leaving at least three feet when passing a bicyclist.

questions.prompt: Nevada's Move Over law requires you to slow down and, if safe, change lanes for:
choices: Stopped emergency vehicles and tow trucks with flashing lights
questions.explanation: The Move Over law protects roadside workers by requiring you to slow and move over for stopped vehicles displaying flashing lights.

questions.prompt: Large trucks are most dangerous to drive near because they:
choices: Have large blind spots and need more room to stop
questions.explanation: Trucks have wide blind spots and long stopping distances, so give them extra space.

questions.prompt: When a motorcycle is traveling in your lane, you should:
choices: Allow it the full lane width and extra following distance
Pass it in the same lane
Tailgate to encourage it to speed up
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane and need extra space because they are small and stop quickly.

questions.prompt: A large truck is making a wide right turn ahead of you. You should:
choices: Stay back and never try to squeeze between the truck and the curb
Move into the truck's blind spot
questions.explanation: Trucks swing wide to turn right, so squeezing alongside risks a serious collision.

questions.prompt: When you see children playing near the road, you should:
choices: Slow down and be prepared to stop
Assume they will stay on the sidewalk
questions.explanation: Children can dart into the street unexpectedly, so reduce speed and stay ready to stop.

questions.prompt: When approaching a pedestrian who is blind and using a white cane or guide dog, you must:
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield and stop for pedestrians who are blind and using a white cane or guide dog.

questions.prompt: To avoid a truck's blind spots, you should:
choices: Avoid lingering directly beside, behind, or in front of the truck
Drive close behind to draft it
Stay beside the cab as long as possible
questions.explanation: If you cannot see the truck driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see you, so do not linger in those zones.

questions.prompt: Before opening your car door into the street, you should:
choices: Check for approaching traffic and bicyclists
Open it quickly so it does not stay open long
Rely on other drivers to avoid it
Open it only on the traffic side
questions.explanation: Checking first prevents your door from striking a passing cyclist or vehicle.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a bicycle at an intersection, you should:
choices: Yield to the bicycle as you would to another vehicle
Always go first since it is slower
Ignore it if it has no lights
questions.explanation: Bicycles have the same rights and duties as vehicles, so apply normal right-of-way rules.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for a driver age 21 or older in Nevada?
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are legally impaired at a BAC of 0.08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: Under Nevada's zero-tolerance law, the BAC limit for drivers under 21 is:
questions.explanation: Nevada applies a zero-tolerance limit of 0.02 percent BAC for drivers under 21.

questions.prompt: Nevada law allows drivers to use a cell phone while driving only:
choices: While holding it to your ear
questions.explanation: Handheld phone use, texting, and reading are banned; only hands-free operation is permitted.

questions.prompt: The fine for a first handheld cell phone offense while driving in Nevada is:
questions.explanation: A first handheld-device offense carries a $50 fine, with higher fines and points for repeat offenses.

questions.prompt: Distracted driving includes anything that takes your:
choices: Eyes off the road, hands off the wheel, or mind off driving
questions.explanation: Visual, manual, and mental distractions all reduce your ability to drive safely.

questions.prompt: Alcohol affects your driving by:
choices: Slowing reaction time and impairing judgment
Having no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Even small amounts of alcohol slow reactions and weaken judgment, increasing crash risk.

questions.prompt: The only effective way to lower your blood alcohol concentration is to:
choices: Wait, allowing time for your body to process the alcohol
questions.explanation: Only time reduces BAC; coffee, food, and showers do not speed up alcohol elimination.

questions.prompt: Prescription and over-the-counter medicines can:
choices: Impair your driving even when taken as directed
questions.explanation: Many medications cause drowsiness or slowed reactions, so check labels before driving.

questions.prompt: What is the BAC limit for a commercial driver in Nevada?
questions.explanation: Commercial drivers are held to a stricter limit of 0.04 percent BAC.

questions.prompt: If you are too tired to drive safely, the best choice is to:
choices: Open the window and keep driving
Turn up the radio and push on
Drink an energy drink and continue
questions.explanation: Fatigue impairs driving much like alcohol, and only rest restores alertness.

questions.prompt: In Nevada, you must turn on your headlights from:
choices: 30 minutes after sunset until 30 minutes before sunrise
Only when it is completely dark
questions.explanation: Nevada requires headlights from 30 minutes after sunset until 30 minutes before sunrise.

questions.prompt: Nevada law requires headlights whenever you cannot see clearly at least:
questions.explanation: If visibility drops below 1,000 feet, you must use your headlights.

questions.prompt: When you turn on your windshield wipers because of rain, you should also:
questions.explanation: Headlights make you visible to others, so turn them on whenever weather forces you to use wipers.

questions.prompt: In dense fog, you should use:
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so use low beams.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle starts to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
questions.explanation: Gentle steering toward your intended path and easing off the accelerator helps regain control.

questions.prompt: Which road surfaces freeze first in cold weather?
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses lose heat from both sides and freeze before other surfaces.

questions.prompt: When driving on wet roads, you should:
choices: Slow down and avoid sudden braking or steering
Drive faster to clear the area
questions.explanation: Wet roads reduce traction, so smooth, slower driving prevents skids and hydroplaning.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning is most likely to occur when:
choices: Driving too fast on a wet road
Driving slowly on a dry road
questions.explanation: At higher speeds on wet pavement, tires can lose contact with the road and ride on water.

questions.prompt: If you are caught in a dust storm with very low visibility, you should:
choices: Pull completely off the road, stop, and turn off your lights
Speed up to get through it
Turn on high beams and continue
questions.explanation: Pulling fully off and turning off lights prevents other drivers from following you into a stopped vehicle.

questions.prompt: When sun glare makes it hard to see, you should:
choices: Slow down and use your sun visor
Speed up to escape the glare
questions.explanation: Reducing speed and blocking glare with the visor helps you keep control when visibility is poor.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb lets the curb stop the car if it rolls.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Pointing the wheels away from the curb lets the tire roll back into the curb if the car moves.

questions.prompt: How close to a fire hydrant may you legally park?
questions.explanation: Parking within 15 feet of a fire hydrant is prohibited so firefighters can access it.

questions.prompt: A curb painted red means:
questions.explanation: Red curb markings prohibit stopping, standing, and parking.

questions.prompt: When parking on a hill with no curb, you should turn your wheels:
choices: Toward the edge of the road
Toward the center of the road
questions.explanation: With no curb, turning the wheels toward the road's edge directs a rolling car away from traffic.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to park:
choices: On a residential street with no signs
questions.explanation: Parking on crosswalks and sidewalks blocks pedestrians and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: A curb painted blue indicates:
questions.explanation: Blue curbs mark spaces reserved for vehicles with disability parking permits.

questions.prompt: Before pulling away from a parallel parking space, you should:
choices: Signal and check for traffic and cyclists
Rely on other drivers to yield
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking traffic prevents collisions as you re-enter the flow of traffic.

questions.prompt: When you leave your parked vehicle on a slope, you should:
choices: Set the parking brake and leave it in park or in gear
questions.explanation: The parking brake plus park or gear keeps the vehicle from rolling on a slope.

questions.prompt: Parking in a space reserved for people with disabilities without a permit is:
choices: Allowed if no one else needs it
questions.explanation: Only properly permitted vehicles may use accessible spaces, and violations carry fines.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for an instruction permit in Nevada?
questions.explanation: Nevada allows teens to apply for an instruction permit at age 15 1/2.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving are required with a driver education course before a Nevada teen gets a license?
questions.explanation: Teens who complete driver education must log 50 hours of supervised driving, including 10 at night.

questions.prompt: How long must a Nevada teen hold an instruction permit before applying for a license?
questions.explanation: The permit must be held for a minimum of six months before licensing.

questions.prompt: A Nevada permit holder may drive only when supervised by a licensed driver who is at least:
questions.explanation: A permit holder must always be accompanied by a licensed driver at least 21 years old.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the Nevada written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: The Nevada knowledge test contains 25 multiple-choice questions.

questions.prompt: What score do you need to pass the Nevada written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the questions correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: During the first six months of holding a license, a Nevada driver under 18 may not drive between:
questions.explanation: Newly licensed teens face a nighttime restriction from 10 p.m. to 5 a.m. except for specific reasons such as work.

questions.prompt: For the first six months of having a license, a Nevada driver under 18 may not carry passengers under 18 unless they are:
questions.explanation: Passengers under 18 are not allowed during the first six months unless they are immediate family.

questions.prompt: If a state-approved driver education course is not available within 30 miles of a teen's home, how many supervised hours are required?
questions.explanation: Without access to driver education, teens must complete 100 hours of supervised driving practice.

questions.prompt: Nevada's Graduated Driver Licensing system is designed to:
choices: Introduce driving privileges gradually to reduce teen crash risk
Let teens skip the written test
questions.explanation: GDL phases in driving privileges so new drivers gain experience under lower-risk conditions.

questions.prompt: How many demerit points within 12 months will result in a license suspension in Nevada?
questions.explanation: Accumulating 12 or more demerit points in 12 months leads to a license suspension.

questions.prompt: Under Nevada's implied consent law, refusing a chemical test after a DUI arrest results in:
questions.explanation: By driving in Nevada you consent to chemical testing, and refusal triggers an automatic revocation.

questions.prompt: In Nevada, seat belts are required for vehicle occupants except children who are:
choices: Under 6 years old in a child restraint
questions.explanation: All occupants must buckle up, while children under 6 must use an approved child restraint instead.

questions.prompt: Nevada law requires a child safety restraint for a child who is:
choices: Under 6 years old and weighs less than 60 pounds
Any child in the front seat only
questions.explanation: Children under 6 who weigh under 60 pounds must ride in an approved child restraint system.

questions.prompt: Driving on a suspended license in Nevada can result in:
questions.explanation: Driving while suspended is a serious offense that brings fines and a longer suspension.

questions.prompt: Demerit points for a Nevada traffic violation remain on your record for:
choices: One year from the date of the violation
questions.explanation: Points are counted for 12 months from the violation date before they drop off.

questions.prompt: A conviction for driving under the influence in Nevada can include:
choices: Fines, license revocation, and possible jail time
questions.explanation: DUI carries severe penalties including fines, license loss, education, and possible incarceration.

questions.prompt: Driving without valid insurance in Nevada can lead to:
choices: Fines and suspension of your registration
questions.explanation: Nevada requires liability insurance, and driving without it brings fines and registration suspension.

questions.prompt: A second handheld cell phone offense within seven years in Nevada adds:
choices: A $100 fine and four demerit points
questions.explanation: A second handheld-device offense carries a $100 fine plus four demerit points.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash that causes injury or significant property damage, you must:
choices: Stop, give aid if needed, and exchange information
Drive away if no police are present
Leave the scene to report later
Continue if your car still runs
questions.explanation: Leaving the scene of an injury crash is illegal; you must stop, help, and exchange information.
